Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Fixed verbal phrase with locative postposition “में” plus compound verb “झोंकणी”; transitive idiomatic construction.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To deceive someone by hiding the truth, especially through clever words or appearances.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: किसी ने धोखो देवो या साँच बात छुपावो।
Usage Contexts: daily speech; family disputes; business dealings; politics; warnings
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Linked to dusty rural roads and market settings of Mewar, where dust in the eyes makes clear seeing impossible; metaphorically, it marks deliberate deception.
First Example Sentence: मेला में व्यापारी मीठी बातां करै, पण भाव में आँखां में धूळ झोंकणी ठीक नीं।
Second Example Sentence: गाँव रा लोगां सूं आँखां में धूळ झोंकणी करसी, तो भरोसो फेर कदे नीं आवे।
Third Example Sentence: कागदां में आंकड़ा बदल के आँखां में धूळ झोंकणी बड़ी खराब बात है।

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Fixed object-complement verb phrase; noun “नाक” plus adjective “ऊँची” plus causative/maintaining verb “राखणी”.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To preserve honour, dignity, and social reputation through worthy conduct.
Usage Contexts: family; ceremonies; education; workplace; community praise
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: In Mewar, the nose is a symbolic marker of honour; keeping it high reflects family prestige, courage, and ethical public behaviour.
First Example Sentence: छोरी ने परीक्षा में नाम रोशन कर्यो, घर री नाक ऊँची राखणी।
Second Example Sentence: मेहमानां आगै मीठो बोलजो, आपां री नाक ऊँची राखणी है।
Third Example Sentence: किसान ने ईमानदारी सूं सौदो कर्यो अर गाँव री नाक ऊँची राखणी।

Idiom (Mewari): कान में तेल घाल कै बैठणो
Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Complex idiomatic clause; locative body-part phrase plus instrumental action “तेल घाल कै” and intransitive verb “बैठणो”.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To ignore advice, warnings, or urgent matters as if one cannot hear them.
Usage Contexts: scolding; parenting; workplace; public safety; humorous complaint
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Oil in the ear evokes deliberate deafness; elders use it humorously or sharply when someone ignores repeated warnings.
First Example Sentence: माँ तीन वार बोली, पण बालक कान में तेल घाल कै बैठो रह्यो।
Second Example Sentence: सरपंच री चेतावणी बाद भी लोग कान में तेल घाल कै बैठा रहा।
Third Example Sentence: काम री तारीख नजदीक है, अब कान में तेल घाल कै बैठणो चालसी नीं।

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Noun phrase idiom with genitive marker “रा”, locative “मुँह में”, and diminutive food item; verbless metaphorical construction.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: An amount so small that it is insufficient for the need.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: जरूरत री तुलना में बहुत थोड़ी मात्रा।
Usage Contexts: food; money; resources; agriculture; household budgeting
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Camels are familiar in Rajasthan’s ecology; cumin is tiny, so the image humorously contrasts a large appetite with a negligible supply.
First Example Sentence: इतरा मेहमानां वास्ते आटू ऊँट रा मुँह में जीरो है।
Second Example Sentence: पांच हजार रुपया सूं दुकान चालावणो ऊँट रा मुँह में जीरो निकळ्यो।
Third Example Sentence: सूखा खेतां वास्ते यो पानी ऊँट रा मुँह में जीरो बराबर है।

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Equative nominal sentence with genitive “री” and comparison “बराबर”; proverb-like fixed expression.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: People often undervalue what is familiar or available at home.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: घर री चीज या अपणा आदमी री कदर नीं करवी।
Usage Contexts: family; talent recognition; local culture; education; workplace
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Common across Rajasthani-Hindi speech zones; in Mewari, it often comments on ignoring local skill while praising outsiders.
First Example Sentence: हमरो गायक शहर में वाह-वाह लेवे, पण गाँव में घर री मुर्गी दाल बराबर समझे।
Second Example Sentence: दादी री दवाई पर भरोसो नीं, बाहर री गोली चाहिये; घर री मुर्गी दाल बराबर।
Third Example Sentence: अपणा कारीगर ने मौका दो, हमेशा घर री मुर्गी दाल बराबर मत मानो।

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Fixed verb phrase; noun phrase “ढोल रो पोल” as object plus transitive verb “खोलणो”.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To expose hidden weakness, fraud, or hypocrisy behind a loud claim.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: छुपी खामी या झूठ सब आगै बतावो।
Usage Contexts: politics; satire; journalism; workplace accountability; public debate
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Drums sound grand from outside, but their hollow body suggests emptiness; the idiom suits Mewar’s oral satire and folk performance tradition.
First Example Sentence: लेखा जांच आवतां ही ठेकेदार रा ढोल रो पोल खुल गयो।
Second Example Sentence: पत्रकार ने सभा में मीठा वादा करणारां रो ढोल रो पोल खोल्यो।
Third Example Sentence: बिना काम री शेखी मारसी, तो एक दिन ढोल रो पोल जरूर खुलसी।

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Adverbial repetition “पग पग” plus locative postposition “पर” and passive participial adjective “बिछेलो”.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To face obstacles at every step in a difficult situation.
Usage Contexts: struggle narratives; migration; farming; bureaucracy; exams
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Walking is a central metaphor in desert and hill landscapes; thorns evoke the physical hardships of rural paths and metaphorical hardship.
First Example Sentence: नौकरी ढूंढता छोरा रा पग पग पर काँटा बिछेला मिला।
Second Example Sentence: बरसात बिना किसान रा पग पग पर काँटा बिछेलो रह्यो।
Third Example Sentence: नवा शहर में भाषा नीं आवे तो पग पग पर काँटा बिछेलो लागे।

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Metaphorical verb phrase; genitive noun phrase “माटी री खुशबू” as object plus compound verb “भूल जाणी”.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To forget one’s roots, homeland, values, or cultural identity after moving up socially.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: अपणी जड़, गाँव, रीति-रिवाज या पहचान भूल जावो।
Usage Contexts: diaspora; cultural criticism; family advice; literature; social mobility
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Mewar’s agrarian identity gives soil emotional force; the phrase frames homeland as smell, memory, and moral belonging.
First Example Sentence: बड़ो अफसर बन्यो, पण माटी री खुशबू भूल जाणी ठीक बात नीं।
Second Example Sentence: विदेस रहो, खूब सीखो, पर माटी री खुशबू भूल जासो तो मन खाली रहसी।
Third Example Sentence: कवि री पंक्ति सुनता ही शहरिया बालक ने माटी री खुशबू याद आ गी।

Idiom (Mewari): हाथ पर हाथ धर कै बैठणो
Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Serial action idiom; repeated body-part noun phrase plus conjunctive participle “धर कै” and intransitive verb “बैठणो”.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To remain inactive when action is needed.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: कुछ करवा बिना खाली बैठो रहवो।
Usage Contexts: workplace; crisis response; farming; household tasks; public service
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: The folded or idle hands image is widely understood in Mewar as a contrast to hardworking rural expectations.
First Example Sentence: आग लागी है, हाथ पर हाथ धर कै बैठणो नीं, पानी ल्यावो।
Second Example Sentence: फसल सूख री है अर तूं हाथ पर हाथ धर कै बैठो है।
Third Example Sentence: समिति ने हाथ पर हाथ धर कै बैठणो छोड़्यो, तब गाँव रो काम आगै बढ़्यो।

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Metaphorical verb phrase; locative body-part noun “जिभ पर” plus object “लगाम” and maintaining verb “राखणी”.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To control one’s speech and avoid rude, careless, or excessive words.
Usage Contexts: family conflict; diplomacy; workplace; teaching; public speaking
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Horse and camel reins are familiar in Rajasthan; controlling the tongue like reins reflects restraint valued in elders’ advice.
First Example Sentence: बड़ों आगै जिभ पर लगाम राखणी सीख, बात री मर्यादा रहसी।
Second Example Sentence: बैठक में गुस्सो आवे तो भी जिभ पर लगाम राखणी जरूरी है।
Third Example Sentence: नेता जिभ पर लगाम राखे तो झगड़ो आधो थई जावे।
